body.title: Fix 'On it' eating replies — reply gate before acknowledgment
body.result: Added REPLY DISCIPLINE block to both system prompt code paths (build_system_prompt and the cached string path). Hard rule: never emit bare acknowledgments like 'On it'/'Fair. On it.' as full response. If acting: act silently. If reply needed: substantive content only. 567 tests pass. Commit: 5e2d8650.
body.description: Igor fires 'On it.' as a canned acknowledgment that replaces real replies. Fix: before generating any acknowledgment response, habit should first ask 'does this turn need a reply?' (intent gate). If yes, route through normal habit scoring to generate a relevant response. If no, suppress output entirely. 'On it' should never be a terminal response — it's a symptom of the response habit firing before the intent/reply-need gate. Related to HABIT_FALLTHROUGH and the reply-need check in the cognition pipeline.
